target.name: Databricks
target.founder: Ali Ghodsi, Ion Stoica, Matei Zaharia, Patrick Wendell, Reynold Xin, Andy Konwinski, Arsalan Tavakoli-Shiraji

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $29.3B - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Databricks, a Series L AI & Software company, is valued at $134.0B following $24.7B in cumulative capital raised. The valuation assessment relies on a peer-median approach applied to eight same-sector, similar-valuation-tier comparables spanning both private and public stages. The peer set includes one public company (Palantir at $400.0B) and seven private companies including OpenAI ($300.0B), xAI ($250.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), and Waymo ($126.0B), among others.

The peer-median method yields an implied valuation of $154.5B across the eight-company cohort, with observed valuations ranging from $29.3B to $400.0B. This approach is applicable given the consistency of sector classification and valuation-tier positioning across the peer universe. The wide dispersion in peer valuations—a 13.6x spread between minimum and maximum—reflects heterogeneity in business model, stage maturity, and investor appetite within frontier AI infrastructure and applications.

Triangulating across available methods supports a valuation range of $131.3B (low) to $177.7B (high), with a median point of $154.5B. Databricks' current $134.0B valuation sits modestly below this median, implying approximately 13% upside to the peer-median estimate. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to peers, defensibility of market position, and the pace of monetization in the underlying AI infrastructure market. Limited publicly disclosed financial metrics for most comparables constrains precision.
